post.description: Creates a new data integration project in the specified space.

requestBody.description: The details of the project to create

get.description: Get a specific data integration project.

post.description: Exports the specified data integration project.

200.description: OK — the JSON-based project was imported successfully.

400.description: Bad Request — for example, if a YAML-based zip is submitted to this endpoint.

post.description:
Imports a data integration project synchronously from a legacy JSON-based `.zip` file.
This endpoint only accepts zips that contain JSON project files (the legacy format). The import is processed synchronously and completes before the response is returned.
Submitting a YAML-based zip to this endpoint returns `400`. To import a YAML-based zip, use `POST /di-projects/{projectId}/actions/import-async` instead.

requestBody.description: The ZIP file containing the JSON-based project to import.

202.description: The project import has started. Poll the returned `actionId` using `GET /di-projects/actions/{actionId}` to track progress.

post.description: Imports a data integration project from a `.zip` file and returns an action identifier for tracking the background import operation. Accepts both JSON-based (legacy) and YAML-based project formats, making it the recommended endpoint for deploying YAML-defined pipeline definitions programmatically. Poll the import status using `GET /di-projects/actions/{actionId}`.

requestBody.description: A `.zip` file containing the project to import. Accepts both JSON-based (legacy) and YAML-based formats.

post.description: Prepares the data integration project and its tasks for execution.
Validates the data integration project and its tasks.

parameters.name: recalculate
parameters.description: Recalculate the bindings if true, otherwise saved bindings are returned.

get.description: Retrieves the export variables for a specific data integration project.

put.description: Updates the export variables for a specific data integration project.

requestBody.description: The details of the export variables to update

get.description: Lists data tasks within a given data integration project.
Get a specific data task within a project.

post.description: Prepares the specified data task for execution.
Recreates datasets in the specified data task.
Registers a request to reload the datasets associated with the specified data task. The reload does not occur immediately; it will take effect on the next scheduled or manual run of the task.

example.summary: Reload selected datasets on next run

post.description: Start a data task on a data integration project.
Stop a data task on a data integration project.

get.description: Returns the state of a specific historical run instance for a data task, including execution progress and any errors encountered.

200.description: Dataset-level state for the specified run instance retrieved successfully.

410.description: Gone — the dataset state for this run has been purged due to retention policy.

413.description: Payload Too Large — the dataset state for this run exceeds the per-response size limit. This is a soft guard against pathologically large tasks; if you hit this consistently, contact support.

get.description: Returns dataset-level state for a specific historical run instance of a data task. All datasets for the run are returned in a single response; this endpoint does not paginate.

400.description: Invalid request. Check parameter formats and filter syntax.

post.description: Returns a paginated list of historical run instances for the specified data task, filtered by the provided criteria.

requestBody.description: Search criteria and pagination options for task run history query.

get.description: Get the current runtime state of a data task

200.description: Returns all datasets for the specified data task

get.description: Returns dataset-level runtime state for a data task

parameters.name: detailed
parameters.description: Specifies whether to include detailed status information in the response. Set to `true` to return detailed information.

get.description: Retrieves the status of an asynchronous operation.

200.description: Validation completed. The response contains a report of warnings and errors found in the project definitions.

post.description: Validates the project definition files in a `.zip` and returns a structured report of warnings and errors. The validation runs synchronously and completes before the response is returned. Use this operation before importing with `POST /di-projects/{projectId}/actions/import-async` to confirm that YAML-based pipeline definitions are correct.

requestBody.description: A `.zip` file containing the project definition files to validate.

AsyncActionLocation.description: URL to check the status of the async action

skipped.description: Number of tasks skipped due to conflicts

space.description: The ID of the space where the project will be created

description.example: This is a new data integration project.

cachedChangesCount.description: Number of changes captured and cached during full load (CDC landing/replication tasks only)

failedRecordsCount.description: Number of records that failed to load (currently only for knowledge marts)

totalProcessedCount.description: Number of records (or docs in knowledge marts) were loaded.

ddlCount.description: Number of DDL statements executed during the last run

deleteCount.description: delete portion of totalProcessedCount. Only available for some task types

insertCount.description: Insert portion of totalProcessedCount. Only available for some task types

updateCount.description: update portion of totalProcessedCount. Only available for some task types

totalProcessedCount.description: Total number of changes/DMLs applied to the dataset

incomingChangesCount.description: Number of incoming changes for the dataset. Only relevant for 'Iceberg Storage' and 'Streaming Transform' tasks.

unoptimizedRecordsCount.description: Number of records that are queryable via the view, but not yet merged into optimized Iceberg partitions. Only relevant for 'Iceberg Storage' and 'Streaming Transform' tasks.

cdcStatus.description: Change Data Capture state for the dataset, tracking incremental changes applied and any errors.

lastProcessed.description: Timestamp of the latest source record inserted into the target dataset.

parseIssueCount.description: Number of records that had parsing issues

recordsWrittenCount.description: Total number of records written to the dataset

totalProcessedCount.description: Total number of processed changes for the dataset

recordsFilteredCount.description: Total number of records filtered out and not written to the dataset

unoptimizedRecordsCount.description: Queryable records pending merge into optimized Iceberg partitions.

streaming.description: Real-time streaming state for the dataset, tracking record processing and transformation statistics.

sourceName.description: Original name of the dataset, relevant only for data movement tasks

dataReadiness.description: Is the data ready for use?

DataTaskDatasetState.description: Represents the execution state of a single dataset within a task run, including full load, CDC, and streaming progress.

errors.description: List of errors encountered during the last run

endTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the task instance ended

gatewayId.description: For tasks that run on a gateway, this is the id of the gateway

gatewayName.description: For tasks that run on a gateway, this is the name of the gateway

datasetCount.description: Total number of datasets produced by the task, including ones in error

gatewayTaskName.description: For tasks that run on a gateway, this is the internal name of the task on the gateway

dataTaskUpdatedTo.description: The latest point in time the data reflects, based on updates from the source system.

lakehouseClusterId.description: For lakehouse storage tasks, this is the id of the cluster where the task runs

liveViewsUpdatedTo.description: The latest point in time the live views reflect, based on updates from the source system.

datasetsInErrorCount.description: Count of datasets that encountered errors

lakehouseClusterName.description: For lakehouse storage tasks, this is the name of the cluster where the task runs

general.description: Overall task execution statistics including dataset counts, data freshness timestamps, and infrastructure details.

traceId.description: Trace identifier for the last run, useful for diagnostics and support

errorCount.description: Number of datasets that have failed full load in this task run

queuedCount.description: Number of datasets that are queued for full load in this task run

loadingCount.description: Number of datasets that are currently being loaded in this task run

completedCount.description: Number of datasets that have completed full load in this task run

fullLoad.description: Statistics for the full load phase of the task run, tracking dataset processing progress.

incomingChangesCount.description: Number of incoming changes. Only relevant for 'Iceberg Storage' and 'Streaming Transform' tasks.

cdcStatus.description: Change Data Capture status information for tasks performing incremental updates, including latency and processing counts.

startTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the task instance started

errorCount.description: Number of streaming datasets that have encountered errors

queuedCount.description: Number of streaming datasets that are queued

runningCount.description: Number of streaming datasets that are currently running

streaming.description: Real-time streaming statistics for tasks that continuously process data streams.

relatesToRecordsTo.description: This batch ends with operational source changes from this time.

relatesToRecordsFrom.description: This batch starts with operational source changes from this time.

lastBatchOfChanges.description: Statistics for the most recent batch of changes processed during the task run, including timing and throughput metrics.

DataTaskInstanceState.description: Represents the execution state of a task instance, including progress metrics, errors, and operation-specific statistics.

DataTaskRuntimeState.description: Represents the current or historical execution state of a data task, including progress information, error details, and dataset-level statistics.

DiExportMode.description:
Defines the export format for the project files.
- 'LEGACY': Exports a ZIP of the previous JSON files. Deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
- 'MINIMAL': Exports a ZIP of the new YAML files, including only non-default attribute values.
- 'ALL': Exports a ZIP of the new YAML files, including all attributes.

limit.description: Maximum number of runs to return.

lastId.description: Cursor for paging. Pass the `runId` of the last item from the previous response to fetch the next page; omit on the first request.

filters.description: Field filters to apply to the search.

DiSearchTaskRunHistoryReq.description: Request parameters for searching task run history, including filter criteria and pagination options.

runs.description: List of task run instances matching the search.

lastId.description: Identifier of the last run in this page. Pass this value back as `lastId` in the next request to fetch the following page.

nextPageExists.description: True when more pages are available after this one; false when this is the last page.

code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling.

title.description: Brief human-readable summary of the error.

detail.description: Detailed explanation of the error and suggested remediation steps.

status.description: HTTP status code associated with the error.

Error.description: Represents a single error condition with details about what went wrong.

errors.description: Array of error objects describing what went wrong.

traceId.description: Unique identifier for this error response, useful for tracking and support inquiries.

Errors.description: Standard error response wrapper containing one or more error details and a trace ID for diagnostics.

pointer.description: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) to the field in the request body that caused the error.

parameter.description: Name of the query parameter or path parameter that caused the error.

ErrorSource.description: Identifies the location of the error in the request.

includeBindings.description: Include bindings in the exported zip file. If not specified, defaults to `false`.

additionalProperties.description: The calculated ID of the variable value, when relevant.

OperationStatistics.description: Breakdown of operations for record-oriented tasks.

PlatformType.description: The platform type of the project. Supported values: - SNOWFLAKE: Snowflake - BIGQUERY: Google BigQuery - SYNAPSE: Azure Synapse - DATABRICKS: Databricks - REDSHIFT: Amazon Redshift - MSSQL: Microsoft SQL Server - FABRIC: Microsoft Fabric (OneLake) - QLIK_QVD: Qlik-managed QVD - QLIK_QVD_CUSTOMER_MANAGED: Customer-managed QVD - QLIK_OPEN_LAKEHOUSE: Qlik Open Lakehouse - NONE: No platform (e.g. replication-only projects)

RecreateTaskDatasetsReq.description: Request body to recreate task datasets.

reloadStrategy.description: Reload strategy (optional, applies to materialized SQL transformations and transformation flows tasks)

selectedDatasets.description: Datasets to reload (optional, if omitted or empty, all datasets will be reloaded).

success.description: Always true when the server successfully registers the request.

ReloadRequestResponse.description: Indicates whether the reload request was registered successfully.

option.description: Task run option for the task (optional, applies to Replication tasks only).

processChangesFrom.description: The value indicating where to resume the process, either a timestamp or an offset depending on the run option (optional, applies to Replication tasks only).

endTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the run ended.

duration.description: Duration of the run in HH:MM:SS format.

startTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the run started.

subStatus.description: Sub-status of the run, when applicable.

errorMessage.description: Error message, if the run failed.

datasetsCount.description: Total number of datasets processed in this run.

originSubStatus.description: Origin-specific sub-status of the run, when applicable.

datasetsErrorCount.description: Number of datasets that encountered errors in this run.

TaskRunItemRsp.description: Represents a single historical task run instance with execution status, timing, and error information.

value.description: Values to match. For `BETWEEN` on `PERIOD`, supply exactly two ISO-8601 timestamps.

TaskRunSearchFilter.description: Specifies a single filter criterion to apply when searching task run history.

TaskRunSearchFilterField.description: The run-history field to filter on.

TaskRunSearchFilterOperator.description:
Filter operator.
- `IN` / `NOT_IN`: exact match against the values list (recommended for ID, STATUS, SUB_STATUS).
- `BETWEEN`: only valid with the `PERIOD` field. The `value` list must contain two ISO-8601 timestamps (start, end).

TaskRunStatus.description: Execution status of a task run instance.

TaskSelectionList.description: Array of tasks to prepare. Leave empty to trigger project-level orchestration using built-in logic (same as in the user interface).

path.description: Path to the field or file in the project definition where the issue was found.

reason.description: Human-readable description of the validation finding.

ValidateProjectDefinitionsReport.description: A single validation finding, including its severity, a human-readable description of the issue, and the location in the project definition where it was found.

ValidateProjectDefinitionsReportLevel.description: Severity of the validation finding. `WARNING` indicates a potential issue that does not block import; `ERROR` indicates a critical issue that must be resolved before importing.

reports.description: List of validation findings. An empty array indicates no issues were found.

region.description: The region the tenant is hosted in

tenant.description: Name of the tenant that will be called
